must_detect.matched_text: Purpose and lawful basis: responding to investor inquiries, scheduling ESG briefing sessions, and conducting pre‑employment screening as permitted by PIPA and related guidance from the FSC/FSS.
Cybersecurity: operational incident logs are stored in Korea and used for defense; public advisories may reference past, already‑announced issues without internal detail.

Garam Renewables Co., Ltd. (Korean issuer) — Investor Relations and Deal Support Privacy & Retention Notice.
We process personal information under PIPA limited to living‑individual data needed for IR queries, site visits, and employment applications; typical fields: name, role, company, business email, and masked resident number (Resident No: XXXXXX-XXXXXXX) where required by law.
We do not request wallet keys, full resident registration numbers, or unsupported identifiers, and any forms showing placeholders like 000000-0000000 are instructional only.
For FSCMA compliance, we maintain an insider list and apply blackout and information‑barrier procedures; the attached training schedule on “crypto” and “cyber incident tabletop” is educational and not a disclosure of any event.
Data sources: self‑submitted web forms, IR inbox ir@garamrenewables.example.kr, and vendor due‑diligence portals operated in Korea.
Purpose and lawful basis: responding to investor inquiries, scheduling ESG briefing sessions, and conducting pre‑employment screening as permitted by PIPA and related guidance from the FSC/FSS.
Retention: IR correspondence retained 3 years; employment records per statutory period; logs from security awareness exercises (e.g., phishing drill) retained 90 days; non‑required resident‑number copies deleted or irreversibly hashed on receipt.
Cross‑border transfer: we do not transfer resident‑number data outside Korea; other IR contact data may be processed by service providers in APAC subject to standard safeguards and contracts.
Cybersecurity: operational incident logs are stored in Korea and used for defense; public advisories may reference past, already‑announced issues without internal detail.
Commercial terms: marketing drafts may describe hypothetical “green PPA” terms for ESG education; they are not binding offers and contain no current deal specifics.
Positive MNPI control: until KRX disclosure, the draft term sheet for a pilot crypto‑custody service with a domestic partner is restricted MNPI and access is limited to named teams on the insider list.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
